records.ety: [L. severus; perhaps akin to Gr. <?/<?/<?/ awe, <?/<?/<?/ revered, holy, solemn, Goth. swikns innocent, chaste: cf. F. svre. Cf. Asseverate, Persevere.]

defs: Serious in feeling or manner; sedate; grave; austere; not light, lively, or cheerful.
quotes: Your looks alter, as your subject does, From kind to fierce, from wanton to severe.

defs: Very strict in judgment, discipline, or government; harsh; not mild or indulgent; rigorous; as, severe criticism; severe punishment.
quotes: Come! you are too severe a moraler.
Let your zeal, if it must be expressed in anger, be always more severe against thyself than against others.

defs: Rigidly methodical, or adherent to rule or principle; exactly conformed to a standard; not allowing or employing unneccessary ornament, amplification, etc.; strict; -- said of style, argument, etc.
quotes: The Latin, a most severe and compendious language.

defs: Sharp; afflictive; distressing; violent; extreme; as, severe pain, anguish, fortune; severe cold.
Difficult to be endured; exact; critical; rigorous; as, a severe test.
